title: Observation
description: A raw or lightly processed factual market event. Observations should avoid interpretation and preserve source provenance.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this record was created.

as_of.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the underlying source data represents.

symbol.description: Ticker, token, market ticker, or instrument identifier when applicable.

source.description: Adapter or data source that produced the observation. Examples: yahoo, kalshi, gecko, dex, sec, fred, fng, tradingview.

source_command.description: Command or tool invocation used to produce the data, when available.

observation_type.description: Controlled-ish factual event type. Examples: return, relative_return, volume_ratio, filing_released, orderbook_move, liquidity_level.

lookback.description: Window used for the observation. Examples: 15m, 24h, 20d.

value.description: Observed value. Use a number where possible; strings and booleans are allowed for event facts.

unit.description: Unit for value. Examples: percent, price, probability, usd, ratio, count, boolean.

comparison_symbol.description: Peer or benchmark symbol for relative observations.

threshold.description: Threshold used to flag the observation, if any.

raw.description: Small source payload excerpt needed for auditability. Do not dump huge responses here.
